textPlain: Meanwhile Milei got involved in a major crypto scandal https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/$Libra_cryptocurrency_scanda... and nothing really happened to him.I understand those are different circumstances, Milei having no such tech skills, I just find it ironic.

BTW, "LaLiga" Spanish operator mentioned in the article as the one promoting the arrest of this guy in Argentina is the same mentioned this past weeks as getting Spanish ISPs to block wide ranges of IP addresses (specially Cloudflare) each time there's a football match on TV:  - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45358433
  - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45195520
  - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45323856
If he writes good code I dont care what his personal life before working for me entails, long as he isnt doing illegal stuff on company resources.

If you're operating e-sports teams and want to build/market a new video livestreaming platform for fans, outside of the walled gardens of YouTube and Twitch and in a cost-efficient manner... bringing on an outspoken and talented entrepreneur who built that exact operation makes all the sense in the world!And from https://9z.gg/equipos I see that one of their teams focuses on a racing simulator... so it seems the subject of this article is one of the few people in history to successfully reach the endgame of that legendary PSA that admonished viewers "You Wouldn't Download A Car." :)https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HmZm8vNHBSU
from jail to getting hired by a slop factory meat grinder that pays like shit.his punishment continues

Trump and his wife both did the same thing on day one of his second term. No one cares. At least Milei faced some public outrage, but Trump has a cult that will defend him in literally any circumstances.
I feel like that's true for most 'stuff', but there are a few exceptions where I will refuse to hire an individual based on things they've done with their personal lives.
define "doing illegal stuff". Piracy is such a weird grey area, especially for companies

>long as he isnt doing illegal stuff on company resources.Previous criminality is one of the most reliable indicators of future criminality.Of course this depends on the crime, but it is absurd to believe that someones past crimes do not reflect on their likelihood to commit crimes again.

Sure, in theory.
But then he is working on some important feature, and the code is on his laptop, which gets confiscated during a raid and now your access to the person and their in-process work is gone. Yeah, I know, code check-ins, documentation, blah blah blah. Totally the strong suit of the typical uber-hacker type /s.The work/life boundaries are heavily blurred these days in even the best cases. Something like this, their personal life as a real potential to impact your business/product/brand.
That aged poorly, I absolutely would download a car.
Obligatory link to "You wouldn't steal a font" https://fedi.rib.gay/notes/a6xqityngfubsz0f (HN discussion https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=43775926 | 1401 points | April 2025 | 407 comments)
